title: Dev Worktree Lifecycle + git Identity
summary: Isolated, correctly authenticated working directory for the dev role. Package internal/worktree provides git fetch origin, git worktree add from origin/main, local git config (env-based credential helper, user.name/user.email from bot identity), worktree_created event, and cleanup (git worktree remove + branch deletion).

stakeholder_intent.goal: Create an isolated, correctly authenticated worktree for the dev role to implement the issue
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Ensures the dev works in a clean branch from main, with proper git identity (bot, not human), and no credentials stored on disk. The worktree is disposable and cleaned up on success.
stakeholder_intent.trigger: After collision check passes (I1.3), the runner calls worktree.Create for the dev role
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: A git worktree exists at ~/.golemic/<project>/worktrees/issue-<N> with correct git config, branch golemic/issue-<N> based on origin/main, and a worktree_created event is written

in_scope: git fetch origin + git worktree add <path> -b golemic/issue-<N> origin/main
Local git config: env-based credential helper (reads GH_TOKEN, no token on disk)
Local git config: user.name and user.email from bot identity (via gh api user with the respective token)
worktree_created event with path, branch, baseSha, role fields
Cleanup function: git worktree remove + local branch deletion (called only on success)
out_of_scope: Remote branch deletion (handled by PR close / cleanup)

preconditions: Executor interface for git commands is injectable
Bot identity is known (via gh api user with the dev token)

business_rules.rule: The worktree is created from origin/main, not from any local branch
business_rules.outcome: git worktree add <path> -b golemic/issue-<N> origin/main; the new branch tracks origin/main

business_rules.rule: The credential helper is set to read GH_TOKEN from the environment, not from disk
business_rules.applies_when: Configuring the worktree's local git config
business_rules.outcome: git config credential.helper '!f() { echo username=x-access-token; echo password=$GH_TOKEN; }; f' set in the worktree's local config

business_rules.rule: user.name and user.email are set to the bot identity determined via gh api user with the dev token
business_rules.applies_when: Configuring the worktree's local git config
business_rules.outcome: git config user.name and user.email set to the login name from gh api user

business_rules.rule: Cleanup (git worktree remove + local branch deletion) is called only on success outcome
business_rules.outcome: git worktree remove <path> and git branch -D golemic/issue-<N> are executed

business_rules.rule: On any error during worktree creation, the worktree is not cleaned up (left for debugging)
business_rules.outcome: Partial worktree may exist; left in place for manual cleanup

business_rules.rule: baseSha is captured from origin/main before the worktree is created
business_rules.outcome: git rev-parse origin/main is run to capture baseSha; stored in the worktree_created event

interfaces.name: Worktree Creator
interfaces.operation: Create(repoRoot, golemicDir, runId, issueNumber, botLogin, executor, eventWriter) error

inputs.name: repoRoot
constraints: Must be the git root of the host repo

inputs.name: botLogin
constraints: Must be the GitHub login of the dev bot
inputs.source: gh api user with dev token

inputs.name: eventWriter
constraints: Must be a valid event log writer for this run

integration_contracts.idempotency: Not idempotent; fails if worktree already exists

integration_contracts.operation: Set worktree-local git config: credential.helper, user.name, user.email
integration_contracts.request_contract: git config --local <key> <value> (run inside the worktree directory)

integration_contracts.transport: gh api user via Executor.RunWithEnv with GH_TOKEN
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: Non-zero exit → 'failed to get bot identity'

state_changes.precondition: Branch golemic/issue-<N> does not exist locally

changes.value_source: worktree_created event with path, branch, baseSha, role: dev

security.authentication: Dev token (GH_TOKEN) is used for gh api user and for git push via env-based credential helper
authorization_rules: The credential helper reads GH_TOKEN from the environment, never from disk
user.name/user.email are set to the bot identity, not the human's
audit_requirements: worktree_created event records path, branch, baseSha, and role

architecture_constraints: All git commands go through the injectable Executor interface
Credential helper must be env-based, not file-based (§2.4)
Cleanup is only called on success outcome (I1.10)
forbidden_changes: Do not store tokens on disk
Do not call cleanup on failure paths
Do not create worktree from a local branch (must be origin/main)
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Single-threaded CLI; no concurrency concerns. The worktree path is deterministic (issue-<N>), so a second run for the same issue would fail the collision check (I1.3) before reaching this code.

acceptance_scenarios.title: Worktree created with correct git command sequence
given: A fake executor that records all git commands
Valid repoRoot, golemicDir, runId, issue number, and bot login
when: worktree.Create is called for the dev role
then: git fetch origin is called first
git worktree add <path> -b golemic/issue-<N> origin/main is called
git config calls set credential.helper, user.name, user.email in the worktree
git rev-parse origin/main is called to capture baseSha

acceptance_scenarios.title: worktree_created event is written with correct fields
then: An event of type worktree_created is written to the event log
The event payload contains path, branch (golemic/issue-<N>), baseSha, and role (dev)

acceptance_scenarios.title: Cleanup removes worktree and local branch
given: A worktree exists at the expected path

acceptance_scenarios.title: Cleanup is not called on error during worktree creation
given: A fake executor that fails on git worktree add
then: No cleanup (git worktree remove) is called
The worktree may exist in a partial state

test_cases: Correct git command sequence for worktree creation
user.name and user.email are set from bot login
Cleanup calls git worktree remove and git branch -D
Cleanup is not called on creation failure
Git command failures return appropriate errors
non_functional_requirements: All git commands go through the injectable Executor interface
definition_of_done: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./... is green
Credential helper is env-based, matching §2.4 specification

decision_log.decision: Use env-based credential helper: '!f() { echo username=x-access-token; echo password=$GH_TOKEN; }; f'
decision_log.rationale: Specified verbatim in §2.4: 'credential.helper = "!f() { echo username=x-access-token; echo password=$GH_TOKEN; }; f"'

decision_log.decision: Determined at runtime via gh api user with the role-specific token
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'user.name/user.email der Bot-Identität (via gh api user mit dem jeweiligen Token ermittelt)'

decision_log.decision: Cleanup is called only on success outcome, not on errors
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'Nur bei success gerufen (§2.11)' and 'Worktrees bleiben bei Fehlern stehen'

decision_log.decision: Worktree package uses the same Executor interface as preflight
decision_log.rationale: internal/preflight defines the injectable Executor interface; worktree commands will use it

decision_log.decision: Worktree package uses the production osExecutor in main.go for real git operations
decision_log.rationale: osExecutor is the production implementation of the Executor interface, used by preflight and reused by worktree

codebase_evidence.finding: Executor interface with Run and RunWithEnv methods exists; worktree commands will use this interface
Production osExecutor implements the Executor interface; used by preflight, usable by worktree
